system:
You are the Watchman of a legal AI lighthouse. Documents arrive in the watched folder; you decide what happens next. You read the filename and the first ~1500 characters, nothing more. Your job is triage, not analysis.

Output a single JSON object with EXACTLY these fields:

{
  "documentType": "jv" | "nda" | "employment" | "lease" | "loan" | "saas" | "policy" | "other",
  "jurisdiction": "string — see jurisdiction rules below",
  "confidence": number,
  "urgency": "routine" | "elevated" | "crit
user:
FILENAME: vnueinc_20150914_8.txt

=== CLIENT (the reviewer's home context — DO NOT copy into jurisdiction) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs

=== DOCUMENT TEXT (the source of facts — jurisdiction comes FROM HERE) ===
Exhibit 10.1   PROMOTION AGREEMENT   This Promotion Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into effective September 10, 2015 between BookingEntertainment.com ("Promoter") of 275 Madison Avenue, 6t h Floor, New York, NY 10016 and VNUE, Inc., ("VNUE"), a Nevada corporation quoted on the OTCMarkets, with offices at 104 West 29th Street 11th Floor, New York, NY 10001.   WHEREAS, Promoter has substantial business experience in the music industry, and since 1996 has built thousands of relationships with artists, entertainment venues and other industry professionals, while delivering turn-key fully produced live concerts worldwide for almost 20 years; and   WHEREAS, VNUE believes Promoter can provide valuable Promotion services related to rapidly securing contracts with multiple venues with which Promoter has existing relationships.   NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the representations, covenants and agreements hereinafter set forth, and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, VNUE and the Promoter agree as follows:   Independent Contractor. Nothing contained herein or any document executed in connection herewith, shall be construed to create an employer-employee, partnership or joint venture relationship between VNUE and Promoter. Promoter is an independent contractor and not an officer, director, affiliate, insider, employee or agent of VNUE or any of its subsidiaries or affiliates. Promoter has no authority to, and will
=== END DOCUMENT TEXT ===

Return your JSON triage decision now. Jurisdiction must come from DOCUMENT TEXT above, never from CLIENT.

}

rationale: The document is a Promotion Agreement between two independent contractors (Promoter and VNUE) with business experience in the music industry.

- Operator vs non-operator dynamics: who controls, who pays, who carries the risk
- Cash calls, dilution mechanics, and dilution rate (what happens to a participant who can't fund?)
- Reserved Matters / supermajority list (is the non-operator's veto meaningful?)
- Sole risk operations + the buy-back economics
- Liability caps and th

CLIENT'S CONCERNS: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
DOCUMENT TYPE: jv
CLIENT'S ROLE: risk-conscious participant

- [TENTATIVE] [PREC-ad970c87] Termination Risk Pattern — The agreement lacks clear provisions for handling capital calls, potential participant withdrawals, and asset distribution upon termination, leaving room for ambiguity regarding fi (effectiveness 23%, seen 4x)
- [TENTATIVE] [PREC-d8b0e91c] Capital Call Risk Pattern — Lack of clarity on how capital contributions are handled, what constitutes 'necessary and expected costs,' and how expenses will be allocated, potentially leading to disputes.  (effectiveness 23%, seen 4x)

=== CLAUSE 1: Section ===
1. Promotion Services. Promoter to provide the following services to VNUE in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement:   A. VNUE hereby engages the Promoter as an Independent Contractor to secure contracts for VNUE with Thirty (30) music venues.   B. The Promoter will provide certain skills, expertise, experience and abilities developed as global leader in the music and entertainment business over two decades.   C. The Promoter will consult and work with the Directors and Officers of VNUE concerning matters relating to business development and other matters deemed necessary to perform the Promotion Services.   Section
=== END CLAUSE 1 ===

=== CLAUSE 2: Section ===
2. Promoter's Fee. For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter i) Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Thirty (30) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer:   Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows:



Source: VNUE, INC., 8-K, 9/14/2015







  For every Five (5) music venues that sign a contract with VNUE, Six Hundred Thousand (600,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter;   and   If Ten (10) music venues sign a contract with VNUE before January 16, 2016, Promoter will receive an additional bonus of Three Hundred Thousand (300,000) shares of VNUE common stock.   Section
=== END CLAUSE 2 ===

=== CLAUSE 12: Section ===
12. Non-exclusion. It is understood that VNUE does not agree to use BookingEntertainment.com exclusively as its Promoter, and that Promoter shall not be held liable for the actions of third parties which may also be providing the same or similar services during the term of this Agreement. Likewise, Promoter is free to contract for services to be performed for other public and private companies while under contract with VNUE, subject to the terms of this Agreement.   Section
=== END CLAUSE 12 ===

13. Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors, heirs and assigns. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada without giving effect to choice of law doctrine. The party in violation of any of the provision agrees to pay to the injured party all court fees, attorney fees, charges and expenses as are deemed fair by the court. Each party hereto consents to personal jurisdiction in Nevada and voluntarily submits to its jurisdiction in any action or proceeding with respect to this Agreement. You are the senior partner closing out a contract review. You have just received per-clause analyses from your team. Produce the executive summary.

**CRITICAL FACTUAL DISCIPLINE — read this twice:**

The CLIENT block you will receive (company, industry, jurisdiction, concerns) tells you HOW STRICTLY to read the document. It is the client's stance, not the document's facts. The summary, documentType, and risks must describe what is in the DOCUMENT — not what the client does or where the client i

=== DOCUMENT (the source of facts) ===
Filename: vnueinc_20150914_8.txt

=== CLIENT STANCE (use ONLY for risk framing — NOT as document facts) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
NOTE: the client may or may not be a party to this document. Do not name the client as a party unless the per-clause findings below explicitly say so.

=== PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS FROM THE TEAM (the source of truth for parties, jurisdiction, subject matter) ===
(Ranked top 10 of 16 clauses by severity. The other clauses had lower-severity findings and are still in the audit trail.)

cl 4 (Section) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause addresses the non-operator's right to perform services and their obligation regarding confidential information, but it lacks detail on how disputes will be resolved or what constitutes 'confidential information'. This could lead to ambiguity and potential for conflict.
  [major] The clause lacks detail on how disputes regarding the non-operator's right to perform services and their obligations regarding confidential information will be resolved. This could lead to ambiguity and potential for conflict.

cl 12 (Section) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause clarifies that VNUE is not obligated to exclusively use BookingEntertainment.com as its promoter and allows for the use of other services during the term, potentially impacting the non-operator's control over the project.
  [major] The clause states that VNUE is not obligated to exclusively use BookingEntertainment.com as its promoter, potentially impacting the non-operator's control over the project.

cl 5 (Section) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause lacks clarity on how the joint venture's operations will be conducted, potentially leading to disputes and operational challenges.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on how the joint venture's operations will be conducted, potentially leading to disputes and operational challenges.

cl 6 (Section) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause outlines the initial term and potential renewal terms for the joint venture agreement, but lacks details on liability, capital calls, or other crucial aspects that could lead to disputes.
  [major] The clause lacks details on liability, capital calls, and other crucial aspects that could lead to disputes. 

cl 8 (Section) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause outlines the responsibilities and limitations of the Promoter in relation to their work for VNUE, but it does not address key JV-specific risks such as liability, capital calls, or exit mechanics.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled, potential participant withdrawals, and asset distribution upon termination. This could lead to disputes and financial uncertainty for the non-operator

cl 11 (Section) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause asserts that this agreement is the sole and complete expression of the parties' understanding, potentially limiting future negotiations or agreements on the subject matter.
  [major] This clause supersedes all prior agreements, oral or written, between the parties. This could limit future negotiations and create a risk of unforeseen liabilities arising from those agreements.

cl 13 (Section) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause establishes governing law, jurisdiction, and remedies for potential disputes, but lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled upon termination.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled upon termination. This could lead to disputes and financial uncertainty for the non-operator.

cl 15 (Section) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause defines the execution method for the joint venture agreement, but it does not address critical aspects like risk allocation, liability, or dispute resolution.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on how joint venture risks are allocated and what constitutes a 'single agreement' for legal purposes. This could lead to disputes regarding liability and financial obligations.

cl 1 (Section) — favours operator
  summary: This clause outlines the scope of services provided by the Promoter, but lacks clarity on liability for performance failures and potential disputes regarding compensation.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on liability for performance failures by the Promoter, which could lead to disputes regarding compensation and potential financial losses for VNUE.

cl 2 (Section) — favours operator
  summary: This clause outlines the payment structure for the Promoter's services in securing contracts with music venues, including a combination of cash and VNUE common stock. It lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled upon termination.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled upon termination.

Now produce the executive synthesis JSON. Rules:
  - Parties, jurisdiction, and document type come from the PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS only.
  - If the findings name specific parties (e.g., "Bravatek and COMPANY", "Veoneer and Nissin"), USE THOSE NAMES verbatim in the summary.
  - Empty brackets like "[other party]" or "[venture purpose]" are FORBIDDEN. If a fact is not in the per-clause findings, write "(not stated in document)" instead.
  - Pick AT LEAST 3 distinct topRisks. A single risk that lists many clause numbers as one citation is NOT acceptable — split it into separate risks per clause group.

summary: This agreement concerns a joint venture between VNUE and the Promoter, governed by Delaware law, regarding the provision of services to secure contracts with music venues. The agreement outlines the scope of services, payment structure, and operational details but lacks clarity on liability for performance failures, capital calls upon termination, and dispute resolution mechanisms. This ambiguity could lead to disputes and financial uncertainty for both parties.

clauses.title: 1. Section
clauses.text: Promoter to provide the following services to VNUE in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement: A. VNUE hereby engages the Promoter as an Independent Contractor to secure contracts for VNUE with Thirty (30) music venues. B. The Promoter will provide certain skills, expertise, experience and abilities developed as global leader in the music and entertainment business over two decades. C. The Promoter will consult and work with the Directors and Officers of VNUE concerning matters relating to business development and other matters deemed necessary to perform the Promotion Services.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on liability for performance failures by the Promoter, which could lead to disputes regarding compensation and potential financial losses for VNUE.

clauses.title: 2. Section
clauses.text: For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter i) Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000.00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Thirty (30) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer:   Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows:
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled upon termination.

clauses.title: 3. Section
clauses.text: VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an itemized accounting of expenses incurred on a weekly basis, and VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting.
clauses.concern: This clause lacks clarity on how disputes regarding costs will be handled.

clauses.title: 4. Section
clauses.text: Promoter affirms that Promoter is not restricted from providing services in this Agreement due to any agreement with any other person or entity. Promoter will not disclose to VNUE or use in its work any trade secrets, inventions or confidential information of any other person or entity which Promoter is not lawfully entitled to disclose or use.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks detail on how disputes regarding the non-operator's right to perform services and their obligations regarding confidential information will be resolved. This could lead to ambiguity and potential for conflict.

clauses.title: 5. Section
clauses.text: 5. Place of Work. Promoter may perform the Promotion Services at such locations as Promoter may choose.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how the joint venture's operations will be conducted, potentially leading to disputes and operational challenges.

clauses.title: 6. Section
clauses.text: This Agreement shall commence on September 10, 2015 and shall continue for One (1) Year (the "Term"). At any time prior to the end of the Term, the Parties may agree in writing to extend the Agreement for successive One (1) Year periods (the "Renewal Terms") under the same conditions set forth herein.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks details on liability, capital calls, and other crucial aspects that could lead to disputes.

clauses.title: 7. Section
clauses.text: The work to be performed under this Agreement will be performed entirely at Promoter’s risk, and Promoter assumes all responsibility for the condition of equipment and facilities used in the performance of this agreement. Promoter agrees to indemnify VNUE for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions of Promoter taken in the performance of this Agreement. VNUE agrees to indemnify and hold Promoter harmless for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions, during the term of this Agreement, of VNUE officers, directors, employees, agents or third parties not under the control of Promoter.
clauses.concern: VNUE is exposed to joint and several liability for all liabilities arising from the actions of the promoter. This could expose VNUE to significant financial risk if the promoter's actions are deemed negligent.

clauses.title: 8. Section
clauses.text: All work will be done in a competent fashion in accordance with applicable standards of the profession. Promoter represents, warrants, and covenants the following:   A. Promoter will disclose to VNUE any and all material facts and circumstances, which may affect its ability to perform its undertaking herein.   B. Promoter is an independent contractor acting in the limited capacity as an independent contractor for VNUE.   C. Promoter will not disseminate or share with third parties any material information about VNUE not already contained in a Company report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission, as Promoter acknowledges that such third parties might try to act on such material non-public information by engaging in "insider trading" to the detriment of VNUE.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled, potential participant withdrawals, and asset distribution upon termination. This could lead to disputes and financial uncertainty for the non-operator

clauses.title: 9. Section
clauses.text: Promoter covenants and warrants that Promoter has the unlimited legal right to enter into this Agreement and to perform in accordance with its terms without violating the rights of others or any applicable law and that he has not and shall not become a party to any other agreement of any kind which conflicts with this Agreement. Promoter shall indemnify and hold VNUE harmless from any and all damages, claims and expenses (including, but not limited to attorneys' fees and costs) arising out of or resulting from any claim that this Agreement violates any such other agreements. Breach of this Section shall operate to terminate this Agreement automatically without notice otherwise required by this Agreement.
clauses.concern: Promoter indemnifies VNUE for claims arising from the agreement violating other agreements, potentially impacting the non-operator's rights and exposing them to financial risk.

clauses.title: 10. Section
clauses.text: Any notice or communication permitted or required by this Agreement shall be deemed effective when personally delivered, or sent by certified or registered mail, properly addressed to VNUE or Promoter at the addresses set forth above.   Section
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on specific notice requirements for various events such as capital calls or termination.

clauses.title: 11. Section
clauses.text: It is agreed between the parties that there are no other agreements or understandings between them relating to the subject matter of this agreement. This agreement supersedes all prior agreements, oral or written, between the parties and is intended as a complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the parties. If any provision in this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue in full force without being impaired or invalidated in any way.
clauses.concern: This clause supersedes all prior agreements, oral or written, between the parties. This could limit future negotiations and create a risk of unforeseen liabilities arising from those agreements.

clauses.title: 12. Section
clauses.text: It is understood that VNUE does not agree to use BookingEntertainment.com exclusively as its Promoter, and that Promoter shall not be held liable for the actions of third parties which may also be providing the same or similar services during the term of this Agreement. Likewise, Promoter is free to contract for services to be performed for other public and private companies while under contract with VNUE, subject to the terms of this Agreement.
clauses.concern: The clause states that VNUE is not obligated to exclusively use BookingEntertainment.com as its promoter, potentially impacting the non-operator's control over the project.

clauses.title: 13. Section
clauses.text: This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors, heirs and assigns. In case any provision of this Agreement shall be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of the Agreement shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled upon termination. This could lead to disputes and financial uncertainty for the non-operator.

clauses.title: 14. Section
clauses.text: 14. Review by Counsel. Promoter acknowledges that Promoter has had the opportunity to have this Agreement reviewed by legal counsel of Promoter's choice.
clauses.concern: This clause merely states that the promoter has had their agreement reviewed by legal counsel, but it does not address any specific risks or concerns related to the joint venture.

clauses.title: 15. Section
clauses.text: This Agreement may be executed via facsimile and in counterparts, which together shall constitute the single Agreement.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how joint venture risks are allocated and what constitutes a 'single agreement' for legal purposes. This could lead to disputes regarding liability and financial obligations.

clauses.title: 16. Section
clauses.text: 16. SEC Reporting and Press Release. Promoter acknowledges that VNUE is an SEC reporting public company and that a Form 8-K will be filed announcing the Promotion Agreement, which shall include a copy of this Agreement, and that a Press Release summarizing the Agreement and Promoter's background may be issued as well. Promoter agrees to make no additional public statements or press releases related to VNUE or to this Agreement without VNUE's prior written consent. WHEREFORE, the parties have executed this Promotion Agreement as of the date written above.  VNUE, INC. BOOKINGENTERTAINMENT.COM       By: /s/   By: /s/   Matthew Carona, CEO     Steve Einzig, President
clauses.concern: Promoter agrees to make no additional public statements or press releases related to VNUE or to this Agreement without VNUE's prior written consent.

risks.description: Lack of clarity on how capital calls will be handled upon termination, potentially leading to disputes and financial uncertainty for the non-operator.

The agreement lacks detail on how disputes regarding the non-operator's right to perform services and their obligations regarding confidential information will be resolved. This could lead to ambiguity and potential for conflict.

The agreement lacks clarity on how joint venture risks are allocated and what constitutes a 'single agreement' for legal purposes. This could lead to disputes regarding liability and financial obligations.

recommendations: Issue a formal request for clarification on the capital call mechanics, including how they will be handled upon termination, and seek to resolve any outstanding ambiguities in the agreement.

Demand that VNUE and the Promoter provide a detailed plan outlining dispute resolution mechanisms, addressing liability, and clarifying the process for handling disputes regarding performance failures by the Promoter.
Audit the agreement's provisions on joint venture risks allocation and clarify the legal definition of 'single agreement' to mitigate potential future disputes.
confidenceNote: This document was analysed entirely on-device using a local model. No content was transmitted externally. For complex legal matters, verify findings with qualified counsel.
